story.text: Work strands because the acceptance discipline (no one signs their own line; merge-node; confirm-arc; external review) has hung off PRs — mutable, mid-flight (a single garden report: 13 stranded branches, a run of 'merged PR, but the cut is held'). bdo's reframe and the spine of epic.environments: a snapshot is the natural unit a second party accepts — immutable, named, recorded — the way an atom's identity already IS the sha256 of its bytes, and a deployment is promoting a snapshot from one environment to the next, acceptance attaching to the snapshot rather than the branch. This is the first build of the LOCAL tier in the local->git->github phasing bdo named this session, and the unit that joins the per-atom (content-hash) and per-PR (git) namespaces loop/pull.py made visible as an open gap. loop/snapshot.py is a pure read-only fold (stdlib, no net/git) with one writer (the mint pen): mint freezes each named atom's content-hash and refuses a ghost binding (an atom with no resolvable file); resolve folds a snapshot to its atoms (frozen-hash vs live-hash), its commit, and the DERIVED acceptance of each (read off the existing value-gate/owner-stamp receipts, never re-judged), and one snapshot-level verdict. It composes reconcile.py's content-hash identity (load_atoms) and the pipeline's value-gate/owner-stamp receipts directly, and is a sibling of pull.py's namespace_gap, digest's atoms_on_main/landed_atoms (D-13), and deploy.py's stamped-snapshot shape — reading the same log and the same identity, never re-deriving them (S10); verifying a snapshot's commit actually reached main via digest.atoms_on_main is a named later increment of the join. The S10 teeth catch a snapshot that LIES about its join: a frozen reference to atom bytes that changed reads STALE, an atom with no file reads GHOST, an unjudged/refused atom is not accepted — two locally-fine records (a frozen reference and an edited atom) that refuse to fit, and the fold notices. Value: a frozen named snapshot is the unit that does not strand, and acceptance can attach to it as one thing; the namespace join becomes inspectable from the log alone. The local-deployment face (serving a snapshot, atom.local-preview.v0) and the git/github tiers are later pieces.
